title: Force unwrap on client.user crashes server on unauthenticated access
description: Multiple handler functions use force-unwrap (!) on client.user without first verifying the client is in LOGGED_IN state. If a message arrives at these handlers when user is nil (e.g., due to a race condition or state machine bypass), the server will crash with a fatal error. This is a denial-of-service vulnerability. Affected lines: 828 (receiveUserGetInfo), 2030 (receiveGetSettings), 2062 (receiveSetSettings). The state machine in receiveMessage only blocks messages at CONNECTED and GAVE_CLIENT_INFO states, but once LOGGED_IN, any message can reach handleMessage and be dispatched to these handlers, which assume user is non-nil.
vulnerable_code:
private func receiveUserGetInfo(_ fromClient:Client, _ message:P7Message) {
    if !fromClient.user!.hasPrivilege(name: "wired.account.user.get_info") {
recommendation: Replace all force-unwraps of client.user with guard-let statements: 'guard let user = client.user else { replyError(...); return }'. This pattern is already used in other handlers like receiveDownloadFile and receiveMessageSendMessage.
